EEG001: Generalized periodic sharp-wave complexes at ~1.5 Hz with bifrontal predominance — classic GPD morphology.
EEG002: Rhythmic ictal discharges with clear evolution in frequency and field — electrographic seizure.
EEG003: Periodic sharp waves maximal over the left hemisphere at C3-P3, consistent with LPD.
EEG004: Generalized rhythmic delta activity at 1–2 Hz without periodic morphology, diffuse distribution — GRDA.
EEG005: Bilateral but asynchronous periodic discharges — left > right, independent generators — classic BIPD morphology.
EEG006: Rhythmic delta activity lateralized to the right hemisphere (C4-P4 maximum), no periodicity — LRDA.
EEG007: High-amplitude generalized periodic discharges at 2 Hz with triphasic morphology — GPD with triphasic waves.
EEG008: Brief rhythmic discharge with evolution in frequency (3→8 Hz) and spatial spread — BIRD progressing to seizure.
EEG009: Brief runs of rhythmic theta over the left temporal region, < 10 seconds — BIRD, difficult to distinguish from brief seizure.
EEG010: Posterior dominant rhythm 10 Hz attenuating with eye opening, normal sleep features — normal variant / benign finding.
